metadata.caseName: JM Smart Building Energy Efficiency Program
metadata.companyDescription: JM is a smart building energy-efficiency service provider for central air-conditioning systems. It uses physics-based models and AI algorithms to deliver 20%-30% energy savings. The company is in a rapid growth phase and is shifting from a project-based model to a subscription-service model.

brief.caseDescription: JM is a smart building energy-efficiency service provider for central air-conditioning systems. It uses physics-based models and AI algorithms to deliver 20%-30% energy savings. The company is in a rapid growth phase and is shifting from a project-based model to a subscription-service model.
brief.coreProblem: The core challenge is to close the gap between strategic ambition and execution by improving enterprise sales capability, delivery efficiency, and incentive alignment.
brief.selectionReason: The materials include a proposal, interview notes, and organization diagnosis, making this a strong OGSM case: the growth ambition is clear, but the path from objectives to actions and measures still needs to be structured.

setupDefaults.projectName: JM Smart Building Energy Efficiency Program
setupDefaults.topic: JM Subscription-Service Growth and Operating Model Shift
setupDefaults.goal: Close the gap between strategic ambition and execution by strengthening enterprise sales capability, delivery efficiency, and incentive alignment.

materials.backgroundSummary: JM is benefiting from carbon-reduction demand and the push for operating efficiency, but internal management capability has not kept pace. The business now needs a stronger strategy-to-execution system that links targets, ownership, performance management, and review cadence. Building this operating muscle is especially important for scaling the higher-value subscription-service business.
observationBullets: Sales: there is no dedicated enterprise-sales role, current coverage is fragmented, and teams struggle to capture system-level client demand.
Delivery and product: product stability still needs improvement, field delivery often drifts from plan, and standardized delivery routines are incomplete.
Organization: the structure is not fully clear, cross-functional communication costs remain high, and the use of performance outcomes for promotion or elimination lacks transparency.
Talent: incentives for core talent are not strong enough, hiring speed trails business growth, and employee development paths are still unclear.
Strategy alignment: senior leaders share the same long-term vision, but the annual priority battles and resource allocation rules are not yet aligned.

sampleDocuments.name: Interview-to-OGSM Strategy Extraction.docx
sampleDocuments.summary: Captures the prompt structure, semantic mapping, and JSON output specification for extracting OGSM elements from interviews.

sampleDocuments.name: JM OGSM Integrated Case Pack.docx
sampleDocuments.summary: Includes the case background, observation notes, key issues, and an OGSM draft table for the demo workflow.

keyQuestions.title: Subscription-service growth targets lack executable levers
keyQuestions.question: How can JM secure at least 60 subscription-service contracts this year and support a RMB 40 million recognized-revenue target?
keyQuestions.description: The annual subscription-growth ambition is clear, but it has not yet been translated into quarterly pacing, ownership, and execution moves.
keyQuestions.evidence: The plan calls for at least 60 subscription-service contracts and RMB 40 million in recognized revenue.
keyQuestions.strategyHint: Break annual contract, revenue, and margin targets into quarterly targets, named owners, and review checkpoints.
keyQuestions.measureHint: Track subscription contract count, recognized revenue, and net margin.

keyQuestions.title: The path for building channels in core regions remains unclear
keyQuestions.question: How should JM build and activate at least 10 strategic channel partners in Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, and Shenzhen to accelerate penetration?
keyQuestions.description: The priority markets are known, but the partner profile, activation model, and regional playbook are still not defined clearly.
keyQuestions.evidence: The case targets at least 10 strategic channel partners in core regions such as Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, and Shenzhen.
keyQuestions.strategyHint: Design partner qualification rules, joint go-to-market motions, and activation mechanisms for the top regions.
keyQuestions.measureHint: Track strategic partner count, channel-sourced contract share, and regional penetration.

keyQuestions.title: Delivery efficiency and talent development are not strong enough to support scale
keyQuestions.question: How can JM improve the employee lifecycle and onboarding-to-ramp process so that new-hire readiness time is reduced by 30%?
keyQuestions.description: Delivery delays, unstable process execution, and slow talent ramp-up are happening at the same time, indicating that organization capability still lags the growth plan.
keyQuestions.evidence: Field delivery often slips from plan, hiring has not kept up with growth, and employees lack a clear development path.
keyQuestions.strategyHint: Upgrade delivery standards, employee journey design, and role-specific capability-building paths in parallel.
keyQuestions.measureHint: Track new-hire ramp time, on-time delivery milestones, and cross-functional execution efficiency.

keyQuestions.title: Incentives are not tightly linked to business outcomes
keyQuestions.question: How should JM redesign bonus and equity incentives so they are directly linked to OGSM goals and reinforce sales and delivery accountability?
keyQuestions.description: Current bonus and equity mechanisms do not directly reward subscription growth or recognized revenue, which weakens execution ownership.
keyQuestions.evidence: The case explicitly asks how to link bonus and equity design to OGSM targets and motivate both sales and delivery teams.
keyQuestions.strategyHint: Connect incentive pools directly to recognized revenue, delivery performance, and quarterly performance conversations.
keyQuestions.measureHint: Track payout linkage ratio, performance-conversation completion, and initiative ownership metrics.

ogsmTable.objective: Scale the smart-building energy-efficiency subscription business
goals: Reach RMB 30 million in signed value in Q1
strategies: Prioritize long-term subscription deals for large accounts (> RMB 10 million)
Use channel expansion to accelerate growth

ogsmTable.objective: Strengthen organization capability to support execution
goals: Keep regretted attrition of core talent below 10%
Establish a full strategic review mechanism
strategies: Improve employee journey design and culture reinforcement
Redesign incentives and performance mechanisms around business results
measures: General culture handbook completed with 100% coverage
Bonus payout tied to recognized-revenue achievement

frameworkOptions.name: SWOT Scan
frameworkOptions.description: Best for quickly identifying strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and risks.

frameworkOptions.name: OGSM Mapping
frameworkOptions.description: Best for breaking objectives into goals, strategies, and measures.

frameworkOptions.name: Process Audit
frameworkOptions.description: Best for identifying process bottlenecks, collaboration gaps, and efficiency issues.

frameworkOptions.name: Value Chain Pulse
frameworkOptions.description: Best for evaluating end-to-end value-chain performance and coordination issues.

sampleProject.projectName: JM Smart Building Energy Efficiency Program
sampleProject.topic: JM Subscription-Service Growth and Operating Model Shift
sampleProject.goal: Close the gap between strategic ambition and execution by strengthening enterprise sales capability, delivery efficiency, and incentive alignment.

swot.title: The value proposition is not differentiated enough to unlock enterprise demand
swot.description: Clients see limited differentiation versus alternative energy-saving offers, which weakens conversion in strategic accounts.

swot.title: Service delivery cost remains too high
swot.description: Field efficiency is below benchmark, pushing service cost per project above target levels.

swot.title: The partner ecosystem is not yet resilient
swot.description: The current partner model lacks incentive alignment and a repeatable co-sell motion.

swot.title: Critical capabilities still rely on a narrow supplier base
swot.description: Concentration of key technical dependencies increases operational and scaling risk.

ogsm.title: Subscription-service growth targets lack executable levers
ogsm.description: The annual growth ambition exists, but quarterly pacing, ownership, and execution mechanisms are still unclear.

ogsm.title: The path for building channels in core regions remains unclear
ogsm.description: Core market priorities are known, but partner qualification, activation, and regional playbooks are not fully defined.

ogsm.title: Delivery efficiency and talent development are not strong enough to support scale
ogsm.description: Delivery slippage and slow talent ramp-up suggest the organization is not yet ready for the targeted scale-up.

ogsm.title: Incentives are not tightly linked to business outcomes
ogsm.description: Current bonus and equity mechanisms do not sufficiently reinforce subscription growth and recognized revenue targets.

process.title: Role ownership across the delivery flow is unclear
process.description: Handoffs stretch because responsibilities are not fully defined across major delivery stages.

process.title: Standard operating routines are not linked to performance
process.description: Execution checklists exist, but they are not reinforced through performance management.

process.title: The tool chain is fragmented across teams
process.description: Teams still rely on separate files and local trackers instead of a shared operating view.

valuechain.title: Compensation design still reflects the old business model
valuechain.description: The incentive model still overweights traditional project wins and underweights subscription economics.

valuechain.title: Talent review has not produced a clear success profile
valuechain.description: Core roles still lack a shared capability model to guide hiring and development.

valuechain.title: Performance review routines are still weak
valuechain.description: Management dashboards remain conceptual and do not yet drive disciplined business review.

riskTemplates.title: Strategy and execution remain disconnected
riskTemplates.copy: If annual targets are defined without owners, cadence, and review discipline, OGSM will remain a planning artifact rather than an operating system.

riskTemplates.title: Channel growth could outpace delivery capability
riskTemplates.copy: If market expansion accelerates before delivery standards are stabilized, client experience and renewal conversion will suffer.

riskTemplates.title: The incentive system may fail to support the business shift
riskTemplates.copy: If bonus and equity design continue to reflect the old model, teams will not fully commit to the subscription transition.

recommendationPool: Build a single OGSM master plan that breaks 60 subscription contracts, RMB 40 million recognized revenue, and margin targets into quarterly operating reviews.
Stand up a dedicated enterprise-sales motion and pilot a strategic channel-partner model first in the top-tier cities.
Stabilize delivery by defining standard work, role ownership, and review checkpoints for the major implementation stages.
Tie bonus pools directly to recognized revenue, on-time delivery milestones, and completion of quarterly performance conversations.
Use an employee-journey map and capability-building path to shorten new-hire ramp time and strengthen organization capacity.

summary: JM is at a critical transition point from a project-based business to a subscription-service model. The central challenge is not a lack of opportunity, but weak alignment between strategic ambition, execution cadence, and incentives.
The materials point to four priority threads that need alignment first: growth target decomposition, channel build-out, delivery capability, and incentive design.
OGSM is the right lead framework because it can convert annual ambition into quarterly objectives, named owners, and review checkpoints that can be executed.
findings: Strategy: annual growth goals are visible, but resource allocation, priorities, and review rhythm are not yet fully aligned.
Market: core-region channel ambitions are clear, but partner qualification criteria, activation mechanisms, and regional playbooks remain incomplete.
Organization: delivery process, talent development, and performance application still do not form a closed loop strong enough for scale.
Incentives: bonus and equity design are not yet tightly linked to subscription growth and recognized revenue outcomes.

frameworkDetails.title: OGSM Mapping
frameworkDetails.description: This case is well suited to OGSM because the growth ambition is already visible. The real need is to translate that ambition into actions, measures, and operating ownership.

highlights.label: Objective
highlights.text: Scale the smart-building energy-efficiency subscription business

highlights.label: Goal
highlights.text: At least 60 signed projects; RMB 30 million signed value in Q1

highlights.label: Strategy
highlights.text: Prioritize large long-term subscription deals; accelerate growth through channel expansion

highlights.label: Measure
highlights.text: Long-term subscription mix above 50%; channel-sourced contracts at 40%

Objective_Clues: What leadership says absolutely must be achieved
The business situation that must be turned around this year
The end-state description of the business shift
Strategy_Clues: Statements framed as "we will do this by..."
Specific improvement moves proposed for current pain points
Priority directions for resource investment such as channels, product, or talent
Measure_Clues: How the team will know the move is working
Specific operating frequencies such as weekly visit cadence

pain_point_analysis.required_output: Map complaints in the interview such as delivery disconnects into strategy directions such as standardized delivery-system design.
